title: Branching
content:
### What Are Branches?

A branch is a movable pointer to a commit. Branching lets you diverge from the main line of development and work independently.

### Managing Branches

```bash
git branch                          # List local branches (* = current)
git branch feature                  # Create a new branch
git branch -m feature feat-auth     # Rename a branch
git branch -d feature               # Delete a branch (safe)
git branch -D feature               # Force delete unmerged branch
```

### Switching Branches

```bash
git checkout feature                # Switch to branch
git switch feature                  # Modern alternative (Git 2.23+)
git checkout -b new-feature         # Create + switch
git switch -c new-feature           # Same with switch
```

### Branch Naming Conventions

- Use kebab-case: `feature/user-auth`, `bugfix/login-error`
- Group by type: `feature/`, `bugfix/`, `hotfix/`, `chore/`
- Avoid special characters and trailing dots

### Tracking Branches

A local branch can track a remote branch:

```bash
git checkout --track origin/feature
git push -u origin feature          # Set upstream
```

Tracking branches let you use `git push` and `git pull` without specifying the remote.
codeExample:
git branch feature-login
git switch feature-login
echo "new feature" > feature.txt
git add feature.txt
git commit -m "Add login feature"
git switch main
git branch -d feature-login

quiz.question: Which command creates a new branch and switches to it?
What flag force-deletes an unmerged branch?
